experience: During my sophomore and junior years I volunteered as a peer mentor for first-year programming courses in the international school and I co-led weekly help sessions focused on Java syntax debugging and basic data structures. I completed two large team projects in software engineering and databases where I coordinated Git branching policies and documented APIs for teammates. I spent one summer as a research assistant building small Python scripts to clean survey exports and visualize response trends. I regularly presented short demos to classmates and I am accustomed to explaining recursion and Big-O ideas at the whiteboard. I enjoy multicultural teamwork and I can keep calm when many students ask questions at once.
self_evaluation: I consider myself reliable and well prepared for each session and I believe I can balance giving hints without solving homework for students. I want to improve how I give structured feedback on code style and I welcome critique from instructors. I learn quickly from mistakes and I am comfortable reading documentation in English. I see this TA role as a chance to serve younger students while strengthening my own foundations before advanced electives.

experience: I focused heavily on systems programming coursework including memory management and simple shell labs. I have not held a formal teaching job yet but I am eager to start in a supervised lab environment and grow from structured feedback.
self_evaluation: I am quiet but thorough and I prefer writing clear comments in code before I explain ideas aloud. I want to become more confident speaking in front of a group this year. I believe patience and careful listening matter most when peers are frustrated by bugs they cannot see.
